message:
fix(runtime): harden failed-start cleanup, add region tombstones, and fix lease expiration race

The previous failed-start path in CompiledApp only called begin_close/begin_drain
on the root region, which could orphan already-started child tasks and their region
subtrees. Replace with a full cancel cascade via new cleanup_failed_start() that:
- Issues cancel_request on the root region
- Collects the full region tree and explicitly completes any registered-but-unpolled tasks
- Iterates advance_region_state from leaves to root until the tree is fully reaped

RuntimeState now tracks a stable instance_id (monotonic u64) so AppHandle can verify
it still belongs to the same runtime instance. Additionally, a bounded tombstone set
(recently_closed_regions, capped at 4096) lets external handles distinguish "region
was closed and cleaned up" from "region never existed in this state".

Supervision child-start failure now uses cancel_request + advance_region_state instead
of the manual begin_close/begin_drain pair, ensuring previously-started siblings enter
proper cancellation rather than remaining live under a dead supervisor boot.

Lease::release() now checks expiration before allowing release — previously a lease
that expired between the last renewal check and the release call would silently succeed,
leaving the obligation in an inconsistent state.

LogCollector with zero capacity no longer indexes into an empty ring buffer.

Also includes: AppHandle and SporkAppHarness now require `mut` for stop(), formatting
fixes in deadline_monitor tests, timer wheel uses cached max_timer_duration_ns field,
and pool waiter formatting cleanup.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
